review_text: This movie made me start watching horror movies it’s so iconic

8 years late...but still glad to have watched Get Out .. A totally outlandish but original metaphorical storyline, backed by terrific performances by all cast members, my personal fav being Allison Williams as the girlfriend Rose Armitage, she is just brilliant! Watch Get Out for it's amazing performances, moody, eerie atmosphere (even in bright sunshine) ,crackling electric tension in every scene & piece of dialogue & it's befitting climax that will catch you by the throat .
Original and executed so well. We will remember this movie centuries from now.
It’s one of the rare movies I’d rewatch. The mix of shadowy, dark scenes and bright, intense moments gives it a unique vibe.
A solid movie that I enjoyed watching every single minute of it

The film builds tension through atmosphere rather than shock, relying on subtle performances, uneasy interactions, and a growing sense that something is deeply off. Peele shows remarkable control for a debut director, balancing mystery and dread while keeping the audience constantly engaged. Daniel Kaluuya anchors the film with a quiet, compelling performance that draws viewers into the story without excess dialogue or melodrama. The supporting cast adds texture and contrast, helping the world feel grounded while slowly amplifying the unease. What sets Get Out apart is its intelligence. It respects the audience, trusting them to pick up on visual cues, tone shifts, and thematic layers without spelling everything out. The film is accessible, suspenseful, and emotionally effective, making it appealing to both casual viewers and serious film fans. As a critic, Get Out stands out as a modern classic in the thriller genre smart, memorable, and confident in its storytelling proving that tension and meaning can coexist without sacrificing entertainment.

Pretty much a perfect film, it's honestly hard to see a reason to give it less than five stars. It's funny when it needs to be and scary when it has to be. The concept is awesome and the racial themes are so well executed.

Really enjoyed this film. It isn’t super gory or gruesome but has an amazing story that you get to gradually see unfold and understand at the same time as the main character. Beautifully filmed and really well made. Will definitely be rewatching soon.
absolutely genius 🙌
I don't know if I've ever seen a film this unnerving. This makes Get Out extraordinarily unsettling and worth anyone's time.

THIS. MAN. IS. TOO. GOOD. AT. THIS JOB. There were more red flags with this family than a communist march. And it still lingers. And it gets worse the more you think about it all. What if.... this isn't quite as fictional as we hope?
The fact that the policeofficer asked for his id in the beginning and wasn’t allowed to by her tipped it over to 5 stars for me! Brilliant all throughout!!

Get Out, a suspenseful horror blast, with lots of humor and beautiful visuals. Jordan Peele is one of the best comedian turned directors of all time, I love it so much that I saw it 5 times in the theaters. But, overall, I give Get Out a 10/10 or a 5/5 stars.

It's well made, visually nice, artistic, an exploration of true & overlooked assumptions & somewhat tense during the first half. Daniel Kaluuya works hard in his role but it offered nothing in the way of scares. It kills drama & tension when the best friend is involved - very clichéd attempts at humour get to tone-slaughtering levels when he's on screen. It all seems very unsubtle, I'd say it was deeply political but there's too much satire for a message to stick & I'm comfortable in my opinion that this is very overrated. I can understand the timing of this movie being a big factor in its success, tackling the subject of racism in a horror sense but combining the amount of cliches, flat dialogue with a ludicrous plot involving brain transplants making no sense when those who have been given a new brain, can be "freed" from the control - DESPITE HAVING A NEW BRAIN... I can't rate a movie highly because it has strong moral intentions - there's no obligation because of subject matter.

A black dude goes to meet his white girlfriend's parents when he starts to realize that other black people are being hypnotized. The suspense will draw you in, but it started getting a little extra with the body swap thing.

One of the most gripping movies I’ve seen in years. For once, the hype was actually deserved. From the opening scene, it hooks you and never lets go, balancing razor-sharp tension with genuinely smart social commentary. The performances are fantastic across the board, especially Kaluuya, who carries every quiet, unsettling moment perfectly. It’s unsettling, funny, and brutal in the best way, and it sticks the landing without losing its nerve. Easily one of those movies that makes you want to rewatch it just to catch everything you missed the first time.
Terrible. I dont even know what to say. Besides dont watch this movie. Unless you are truly bored and have nothing else to do.
The movie looks like it was designed against white people, had no meanings value in it. That’s my impression of it.
Really good. Definitely worth watching.

I still say to this day that Get Out is an absolute psychological masterpiece. The message hits quietly at first, then builds perfectly throughout each scene. The socially awkward moments? Painfully real. The forced smiles, strange behavior, the weird awkward comments, the “did they really just say that?” energy that makes you wonder wth is going on… Theres scenes that crawl under your skin because they can and do actually happen in real life. No exaggeration, just truth in the discomfort… anyone saying the movie was bad simply doesn’t get the bigger picture OR they don’t like feeling uncomfortable by the truth lol What makes it more brilliant is the balance. It never relies on cheap jump scares; instead, it builds psychological tension that doesn’t get stagnant or boring. The creepiness is always there, the unease lingers, and the twists land perfectly all the way till the end. Idk anyone who hasn’t seen this already but if you haven’t yet then wait no more!
Spike Jonez is overrated af. The plot is rather boring and fantastical in the sense that its over the top fiction.

I don’t like creepy or scary movies usually but this right here is the exception. I do not think I have ever seen a movie with better visual clues. The second time I watched it I realized just how many little details there were. Overall this movie is a 10.

Suspenseful, thrilling, original, unexpected, one of my favorites of all time.

As a horror nerd and long time lover of the genre I can safely say this is one of the top 10 best horror films released in the past 25 years.

Get Out’s whole journey, through every tense conversation, A-plus punchline and shocking act of violence, feels totally earned. And the conclusion is worth each uncomfortable chuckle and moment of doubt. Ultimately Get Out can’t help but ask the question: How would all these white people feel if they could walk in a black person’s shoes for a day? And that’s something worth considering.
i loved this movie, the foreshadowing is amazing.

Had high expectations. Definitely didn’t exceed them, now to understand this movie you need a deep understanding of whats happening. SPOILER: In my opinion an innocent man that got basically manipulated into a psychopathic, seemingly normal family’s house is a creepy concept. The acting was so good you felt it was infront of you. Now even for someone who had higher expectations on the movie, and was a little disappointed. This movie was creepy and had parts that freaks you out. But I didn’t get a chance to experience what everyone else did. Definitely will be re-watching. A great 4 stars for quality as of now

Get Out is a razor-sharp horror movie with a wicked sense of humor and an adroit perspective
A brilliant, genre-defining horror that combines sharp social commentary, tense suspense, and outstanding performances

I think this movie slightly exceeded my expectations. It's not that my expectations were low; in fact, they were high. But the synopsis I'd read didn't interest me that much, but it's highly rated so I decided to watch it. From the start, I liked the movie's music, especially the two opening songs. As for the acting and the characters, I found Chris, played by Daniel Kaluuya, as a likable, unpretentious, and composed individual, the sort of person who genuinely tries to avoid trouble and monitors his conduct. Also Rod, played by Lil Rel Howery, I immediately liked them, and he made me laugh a lot. He gave his character a lot of personality. Overall, I liked all the performances, but especially theirs. As for the plot, I found it to be more interesting than I expected. There were several surprises, things that at least weren't obvious or predictable to the point that I wasn't sure how the whole story would end. That said, sadly we’re used to certain tragedies happening in real life, especially in the final sequence with Chris raising his hands. I’m from Mexico and I feared something very different would happen than what actually did. I don’t know how it took me so long to watch this movie.
Genuinely inventive, scary, and shocking. This was an incredible first effort from Jordan Peele

Overall, this movie really stands out with its great storytelling and strong performances. It’s engaging from start to finish, smoothly balancing moments of humor, drama, and tension that keep you hooked throughout. The characters feel real, and the story is interesting enough to hold your attention without feeling rushed. It’s definitely worth watching if you enjoy this type of genre. The film is well-made, entertaining, and leaves a lasting impression. Even after it’s over, you’ll find yourself thinking about the story and the characters, making it a memorable experience. -flickswithmigs
Instant classic. Thriller and horror wrapped perfectly up for you
One of the best horror films ever imo. Peele hit this rookie effort way out of the park, and has understandably never matched it with his next films.
